steps.title: Identify the right angle using the Pythagorean theorem
steps.content:
Check which sides satisfy $a^2 + b^2 = c^2$:
$$504^2 + 128^2 = 254016 + 16384 = 270400 = 520^2$$The sum of squares of $RS$ and $ST$ equals the square of $TR$, so the right angle is at vertex $S$.

steps.title: Use correspondence to find tan W
steps.content:
Since triangle $RST$ is similar to triangle $UVW$ with $T$ corresponding to $W$, we have $\tan W = \tan T$.

In right triangle $RST$ with the right angle at $S$, for angle $T$:
  • Opposite side: $RS = 504$
  • Adjacent side: $ST = 128$
$$\tan T = \frac{\text{opposite}}{\text{adjacent}} = \frac{RS}{ST} = \frac{504}{128}$$

steps.title: Simplify the fraction
steps.content:
Divide both numerator and denominator by their GCD of 8:
$$\frac{504}{128} = \frac{504 \div 8}{128 \div 8} = \frac{63}{16}$$So $\tan T = \frac{63}{16}$, and therefore $\tan W = \frac{63}{16}$. That matches choice D — D is correct.
